data.content:
### REST API Overview

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rest-api/overview.mdx

Payload automatically generates a fully functional REST API from your Collection and Global configs. The API is a fully functional HTTP client that allows you to interact with your Documents in a RESTful manner with automatic pagination, depth, and sorting capabilities.

```APIDOC
## REST API Overview

### Description
Payload generates a fully functional REST API automatically from your Collection and Global configurations. The REST API provides a complete HTTP client interface for interacting with your Documents in a RESTful manner, supporting all CRUD operations with built-in features like automatic pagination, depth control, and sorting.

### Base Configuration
All Payload API routes are mounted and prefixed to your config's `routes.api` URL segment.

**Default Base Path:** `/api`

### REST Query Parameters
The following query parameters are available across all REST API endpoints:

#### Common Parameters
- **depth** (integer) - Optional - Automatically populates relationships and uploads
- **locale** (string) - Optional - Retrieves document(s) in a specific locale
- **fallback-locale** (string) - Optional - Specifies a fallback locale if no locale value exists
- **select** (string) - Optional - Specifies which fields to include in the result
- **populate** (string) - Optional - Specifies which fields to include in the result from populated documents
- **limit** (integer) - Optional - Limits the number of documents returned
- **page** (integer) - Optional - Specifies which page to get documents from when used with a limit
- **sort** (string) - Optional - Specifies the field(s) to use to sort the returned documents by
- **where** (object) - Optional - Specifies advanced filters to use to query documents
- **joins** (object) - Optional - Specifies the custom request for each join field by name of the field

### Payload SDK
To enhance developer experience, you can use the Payload SDK to query your REST API with improved type safety and convenience methods.

### Collections
Each collection is mounted using its `slug` value. Collection slugs must be formatted in kebab-case.

**Example:** If a collection's slug is `users`, all corresponding routes will be mounted on `/api/users`
```

--------------------------------

### REST API - Collections

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/tools/claude-plugin/skills/payload/reference/QUERIES.md

Endpoints for managing documents within collections via the REST API.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/{collection}

### Description
Find documents within a specified collection.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/{collection}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **collection** (string) - Required - The name of the collection.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **docs** (array) - An array of documents matching the query.
- **totalDocs** (number) - The total number of documents found.
- **hasNextPage** (boolean) - Indicates if there are more pages of results.

## GET /api/{collection}/{id}

### Description
Find a specific document by its ID within a collection.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/{collection}/{id}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **collection** (string) - Required - The name of the collection.
- **id** (string) - Required - The ID of the document to retrieve.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **doc** (object) - The document with the specified ID.

## POST /api/{collection}

### Description
Create a new document within a specified collection.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/{collection}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **collection** (string) - Required - The name of the collection.

#### Request Body
- **data** (object) - Required - The data for the new document.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **doc** (object) - The newly created document.

## PATCH /api/{collection}/{id}

### Description
Update an existing document by its ID within a collection.

### Method
PATCH

### Endpoint
/api/{collection}/{id}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **collection** (string) - Required - The name of the collection.
- **id** (string) - Required - The ID of the document to update.

#### Request Body
- **data** (object) - Required - The updated data for the document.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **doc** (object) - The updated document.

## DELETE /api/{collection}/{id}

### Description
Delete a document by its ID within a collection.

### Method
DELETE

### Endpoint
/api/{collection}/{id}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **collection** (string) - Required - The name of the collection.
- **id** (string) - Required - The ID of the document to delete.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **success** (boolean) - Indicates if the deletion was successful.

## GET /api/{collection}/count

### Description
Count the number of documents within a specified collection.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/{collection}/count

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **collection** (string) - Required - The name of the collection.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **totalDocs** (number) - The total number of documents in the collection.
```

--------------------------------

### Authenticate REST/GraphQL API Request with API Key (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/authentication/api-keys.mdx

This example shows how to make an authenticated HTTP request using an API key with the Fetch API. The `Authorization` header must include the collection's slug, ' API-Key ', and the actual API key. Payload's middleware then assigns the associated user document to `req.user` for access control.

```typescript
import Users from '../collections/Users'

const response = await fetch('http://localhost:3000/api/pages', {
  headers: {
    Authorization: `${Users.slug} API-Key ${YOUR_API_KEY}`,
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### REST API - Globals

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/tools/claude-plugin/skills/payload/reference/QUERIES.md

Endpoints for managing global configuration settings via the REST API.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/globals/{slug}

### Description
Retrieve global configuration settings by slug.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/globals/{slug}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **slug** (string) - Required - The slug of the global configuration.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **global** (object) - The global configuration data.

## POST /api/globals/{slug}

### Description
Update global configuration settings by slug.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/globals/{slug}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **slug** (string) - Required - The slug of the global configuration.

#### Request Body
- **data** (object) - Required - The updated global configuration data.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **global** (object) - The updated global configuration data.
```

--------------------------------

### Call External APIs from a Payload CMS Task (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/jobs-queue/tasks.mdx

This example shows how to integrate with third-party services from a Payload CMS task without blocking the main API. It fetches a document using `req.payload.findByID` and then sends its data to an external API using `fetch`, with robust error checking for the API response.

```ts
{
  slug: 'syncToThirdParty',
  retries: 3,
  inputSchema: [
    {
      name: 'documentId',
      type: 'text',
      required: true,
    },
  ],
  handler: async ({ input, req }) => {
    const doc = await req.payload.findByID({
      collection: 'documents',
      id: input.documentId,
    })

    // Call external API
    const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/sync', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(doc),
    })

    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(`API error: ${response.statusText}`)
    }

    return {
      output: {
        synced: true,
        apiResponse: await response.json(),
      },
    }
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch data from Payload REST API (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/concepts.mdx

This example demonstrates how to make a GET request to a Payload CMS REST API endpoint for a 'pages' collection. It uses the `fetch` API to retrieve JSON data and logs it to the console, assuming the REST API is mounted at '/api'.

```ts
fetch('https://localhost:3000/api/pages') // highlight-line
  .then((res) => res.json())
  .then((data) => console.log(data))
```

--------------------------------

### Local API Pagination Example

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/queries/pagination.mdx

Demonstrates how to use `limit` and `page` options within the Local API to fetch paginated documents.

```APIDOC
## Local API Pagination

### Description

Use the `limit`, `page`, and `pagination` options in your local API queries to control pagination.

### Method

`find` (Local API)

### Endpoint

N/A (Local API)

### Parameters

#### Query Parameters

- **limit** (number) - Optional - Limits the number of documents returned per page. Defaults to `10`.
- **page** (number) - Optional - Specifies the page number to retrieve. Defaults to `1`.
- **pagination** (boolean) - Optional - Set to `false` to disable pagination. Defaults to `true`.

### Request Example

```ts
import type { Payload } from 'payload'

const getPosts = async (payload: Payload) => {
  const posts = await payload.find({
    collection: 'posts',
    limit: 10,
    page: 2,
  })

  return posts
}
```

### Response

#### Success Response (200)

The response includes `docs` (array of documents) and pagination metadata (`totalDocs`, `limit`, `totalPages`, `page`, `pagingCounter`, `hasPrevPage`, `hasNextPage`, `prevPage`, `nextPage`).

#### Response Example

```json
{
  "docs": [
    {
      "title": "Page Title",
      "description": "Some description text",
      "priority": 1,
      "createdAt": "2020-10-17T01:19:29.858Z",
      "updatedAt": "2020-10-17T01:19:29.858Z",
      "id": "5f8a46a1dd05db75c3c64760"
    }
  ],
  "totalDocs": 6,
  "limit": 1,
  "totalPages": 6,
  "page": 1,
  "pagingCounter": 1,
  "hasPrevPage": false,
  "hasNextPage": true,
  "prevPage": null,
  "nextPage": 2
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Define PayloadCMS REST API Method Mappings (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rest-api/overview.mdx

Initializes an object named `api` that maps standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, PUT) to specific REST functions provided by a `config` object. This centralizes API call methods for easier management and invocation.

```typescript
const api = {
  GET: REST_GET(config),
  POST: REST_POST(config),
  PATCH: REST_PATCH(config),
  DELETE: REST_DELETE(config),
  PUT: REST_PUT(config)
}
```

--------------------------------

### REST API Pagination Example

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/queries/pagination.mdx

Illustrates how to use query string parameters for `limit` and `page` in REST API requests.

```APIDOC
## REST API Pagination

### Description

Control pagination in REST API calls by appending `limit` and `page` as query string parameters to your requests.

### Method

`GET`

### Endpoint

`/api/[collection-slug]?limit=10&page=2`

### Parameters

#### Query Parameters

- **limit** (number) - Optional - Limits the number of documents returned per page. Defaults to `10`.
- **page** (number) - Optional - Specifies the page number to retrieve. Defaults to `1`.
- **pagination** (boolean) - Optional - Set to `false` to disable pagination. Defaults to `true`.

### Request Example

```http
GET /api/posts?limit=10&page=2
```

### Response

#### Success Response (200)

Paginated responses include a `docs` array and pagination metadata.

#### Response Example

```json
{
  "docs": [
    {
      "title": "Page Title",
      "description": "Some description text",
      "priority": 1,
      "createdAt": "2020-10-17T01:19:29.858Z",
      "updatedAt": "2020-10-17T01:19:29.858Z",
      "id": "5f8a46a1dd05db75c3c64760"
    }
  ],
  "totalDocs": 6,
  "limit": 1,
  "totalPages": 6,
  "page": 1,
  "pagingCounter": 1,
  "hasPrevPage": false,
  "hasNextPage": true,
  "prevPage": null,
  "nextPage": 2
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Payload Collection for API Key Authentication (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/authentication/api-keys.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to enable API key generation and usage for a specific Payload collection. By setting `useAPIKey: true` in the collection's `auth` configuration, a new interface appears in the Admin Panel for generating API keys for each document, simplifying authentication for external services.

```typescript
import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload'

export const ThirdPartyAccess: CollectionConfig = {
  slug: 'third-party-access',
  auth: {
    useAPIKey: true, // highlight-line
  },
  fields: [],
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Collection Document with Type Inference in Payload CMS (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/local-api/overview.mdx

Demonstrates creating a document in a collection using the Payload CMS local API within a TypeScript environment. This example highlights how Payload's local API calls automatically infer generated types, providing type hints for the data object for improved development experience.

```ts
// Properly inferred as `Post` type
const post = await payload.create({
  collection: 'posts',

  // Data will now be typed as Post and give you type hints
  data: {
    title: 'my title',
    description: 'my description',
  },
})
```
